title: clerodendrum atlanticum
intro: Clerodendrum atlanticum, also known as the 'Wild Forest Bitters', is a plant species native to tropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and sore throats. Herbal preparations of Clerodendrum atlanticum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health.
